reviews.review_title: Fun but environment let's it down
reviews.review_description: I do like this, and it's fun to play. However I do wish there were different environments to play in. I find the black dark location quite depressing and makes me less likely to want to play it. It will be fab if it could be played in sort of an open real world environment with blue skies etc

reviews.review_title: Breakout in vr like no other...
reviews.review_description:
Like classic breakout but done in a way that only can be done in vr, also a great game for new users to vr since theres no artificial locomotion so no sickness, but still has that wow factor with its mechanics and environments...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

I have been playing this game for over a year and a half, and I love this game. But I just discovered two things that makes this game even better! While you are in the game, you can alternate your racket between left and right hand. All of this time I didn't know that! I discovered it by accident and my enjoyment of this game has gone up a level! I love that ability in the game. 

The second thing is the Zen mode. I did notice this from the beginning, but I'm not much into Zen so I immediately discounted it. Recently, just for kicks, I decided to try it. Now I am kicking myself. The difference between Zen mode and the other modes is that there is no life bar, so you don't have to worry about hitting the blue plusses for life or  accidentally hitting the orange skulls which takes your life down. I still get my exercise, but I can go as long as I want and not worry about the game ending abruptly because I lost too much life.

I LOVE this game!
How to you, download music to this game I love this game! Play it now and again, but if I had my music downloaded to it I would play it a lot more. Does anybody know how to download your music to it?

developer_response.body: If you connect your device to a PC, you can browse the internal storage of the device. There you'll find a Music folder. Just create a sub folder named 'Racket NX' and copy mp3 files into it. Now in the game setting you will be able to switch to your own music

reviews.review_description: Fantastic game that's highly underrated,. Been playing for over the last 2 years and always come back for more. Game reminds me of arkanoid a game I loved on the spectrum.
I really enjoyed the physics of it and happy to be in a futuristic setting. A cool game worth playing for a bit of fun with levels.

The overall experience is very satisfying, and the game is well made. You hit the ball with a racket against 360-degree walls to score points, avoid traps, and progress to the next section after completing each stage. You can try predefined levels, an infinite arcade mode, and even multiplayer. I like to jump in and play it once in a while for some quick fun. Just be careful not to accidentally hit your real surroundings, as the gameplay is pretty dynamic and engaging. One small thing that could be improved is the variety of game environments. For example, it would be great if the scene could be configurable or randomly selected from different presets at the start.

Its a great game, but man after I played Underdogs and saw what you can do I cannot unsee that 6 year difference. Please make graphics update. You can sell domes, balls and rackets skins and I will happily pay for it!

In a 360 dome like level, hitting a ball against a wall. Sounds simple right? Anything but that. Different grids and panels take different hitting styles to clear. Took the idea of breakout and made it 360. You can use the controller to spin, but I find it best to turn your body around and get immersed in the game. Physics fell good and a fun arcade like game to burn some time. You can also work up a bit of a work-out if you get into this and play it the way it was meant too. For what it is, this is a very well done game.

Devs, have you guys/other gender/other, other gender/other, other, other gender checked out Bounce Arcade? Five minutes into playing it I thought "this has so many game mechanics that are CLEARLY lifted from Racket NX, it has to be the same development team" dun dun duh...it isn't.
Normally I wouldn't care but Racket NX was the first game I bought years ago for my Quest 2 and I still not only play it but still use it as a tool when I'm trying to get someone into VR and it NEVER fails.

I decelerate the momentum of my body having just sent a fireball through a wormhole with one of my vicious back hands. The spherical neocyberpunk chamber I stand in pulsing with blocks, colours and multiple exit points from which the fire demon is about to emerge at double the speed. My eyes are narrowed, focused, darting from one possible direction to the other, my body coils in anticipation of the comeback. I hear the building whoosh over my right shoulder and turn just in time to raise my hand to my face deflecting the unstoppable menace with the side of my racket. The ball ricochets off of the floor and into the wall extinguishing a few more blocks scoring some valuable yet hardly deserved points. I track the path of the ball, its spin sending it up the curved wall towards the ceiling. As it reaches the apex It’s trajectory curves towards my head, picking up the speed. This time I’m ready. With the level of athleticism that up to this point has only been seen on the grand slam courts my body arches, my hand reaches behind my back and drives towards the ball for a perfectly timed smash. The power of my tennis racket hitting against the metal ball reverberates as a shock wave down my arm and the ball is sent to the other side…..wait why is my hand hurting, what was the crash I just heard and why can I feel fragments under my feet. I slowly pull off my headset, first look at my hand (fine) and and then around the living room. Covered in shards of glass, the light bouncing from one wall to the other, my eyes slowly lift towards the ceiling. The chandelier gone, leaving only an exposed lightbulb swinging gently from side to side before coming to a stop….Months later I had a call from my landlord asking why was the chandelier gone, was I not a fan of his interior decoration style? I mumbled something about tennis and Vr, ”Right” he said. “Replace it.”
Very fun. Many levels with energetic audio and announcer.

reviews.review_title: It's fun but not much of a workout
reviews.review_description: I like this game, but I wanted something to elevate my heart rate. In this case, I spent a lot of time standing around, watching my shot bounce all over the place and rack up points. That would be great, if I were standing in front of an arcade cabinet, but it didn't give me much of a workout. I got a much better workout playing squash in All-in-One Sports.

One of those games where you don’t feel like you’re exercising. I did the trial and am hooked. Not sure I quite understand the scoring, but the graphics are cool and reminds me of being in a Tron-like world. It has single player which I like because I’m not into the multiplayer thing.

reviews.review_title: Very fun and lots of upgrades built-in
reviews.review_description: I've been playing this for 3 days now and it is great! Highly recommend it.

reviews.review_title: So fun; sneaky sweat, money well spent!
reviews.review_description: I have had fun every single time that I've played,  ever since I first bought it. Multiplayer is awesome too. This is money well spent you will get so much play out of this and if you're looking for a workout this is great to throw in there to get you in the mood to have a workout or to get that little bit of remaining energy out of you because it just feels like fun! It looks great it sounds great but I got to complain that it seems to be pretty much abandoned other than the monthly challenge which I make sure I tune in for and I get excited for each month that said I still can't believe that they aren't releasing a bunch of DLCs since this is still one of the absolute faves of all time. Seriously, guys! Do you not like money and fame? Please, give the people want they want!!

I was skeptical about this game at first but decided to give it a try. And let me tell you, I'm so happy I did. The graphics are amazing and the gameplay is so much more fun than I ever expected. Definitely a must buy in my opinion.
I love sport type of games and to be active, it is addictive and I played until my quest died. You should have a good amount of space when playing this game. I was moving everywhere.
I love this game. It has great music to accompany it. A word to the wise, make sure your wrist straps are good and connected.
This is a close second on the fun scale after beat saber. Much more chill yet still engaging.
